{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/23704","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/23704/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/23704/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/23704/events","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/23704","id":1693723241,"node_id":"I_kwDOAA3dP85k9Cpp","number":23704,"title":"BUG: Cannot install numpy on UOS system.","user":{"login":"SmartDev-0205","id":59509767,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjU5NTA5NzY3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/59509767?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/SmartDev-0205","html_url":"https://github.com/SmartDev-0205","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SmartDev-0205/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SmartDev-0205/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SmartDev-0205/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SmartDev-0205/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SmartDev-0205/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SmartDev-0205/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SmartDev-0205/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SmartDev-0205/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SmartDev-0205/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":6359214,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw2MzU5MjE0","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/labels/00%20-%20Bug","name":"00 - Bug","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":9,"created_at":"2023-05-03T09:39:18Z","updated_at":"2024-09-05T04:50:10Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the issue:

I am trying to install numpy on python3.11.
I am using the UOS system.
But I get an error and cannot install it.

#error Unknown CPU, please report this to numpy maintainers with \
            ^~~~~
      error: Command "gcc -pthread -Wsign-compare -DNDEBUG -g -fwrapv -O3 -Wall -fPIC -Ibuild/src.linux-sw_64-3.11/numpy/core/src/npymath -Inumpy/core/include -Ibuild/src.linux-sw_64-3.11/numpy/core/include/numpy -Ibuild/src.linux-sw_64-3.11/numpy/distutils/include -Inumpy/core/src/common -Inumpy/core/src -Inumpy/core -Inumpy/core/src/npymath -Inumpy/core/src/multiarray -Inumpy/core/src/umath -Inumpy/core/src/npysort -Inumpy/core/src/_simd -I/home/uos/OCR/venv/include -I/usr/local/include/python3.11 -Ibuild/src.linux-sw_64-3.11/numpy/core/src/common -Ibuild/src.linux-sw_64-3.11/numpy/core/src/npymath -c numpy/core/src/npymath/npy_math.c -o build/temp.linux-sw_64-3.11/numpy/core/src/npymath/npy_math.o -MMD -MF build/temp.linux-sw_64-3.11/numpy/core/src/npymath/npy_math.o.d" failed with exit status 1

Please let me know what is the solution.

### Reproduce the code example:

```python
I cannot install
```


### Error message:

```shell
#error Unknown CPU, please report this to numpy maintainers with \
            ^~~~~
      error: Command "gcc -pthread -Wsign-compare -DNDEBUG -g -fwrapv -O3 -Wall -fPIC -Ibuild/src.linux-sw_64-3.11/numpy/core/src/npymath -Inumpy/core/include -Ibuild/src.linux-sw_64-3.11/numpy/core/include/numpy -Ibuild/src.linux-sw_64-3.11/numpy/distutils/include -Inumpy/core/src/common -Inumpy/core/src -Inumpy/core -Inumpy/core/src/npymath -Inumpy/core/src/multiarray -Inumpy/core/src/umath -Inumpy/core/src/npysort -Inumpy/core/src/_simd -I/home/uos/OCR/venv/include -I/usr/local/include/python3.11 -Ibuild/src.linux-sw_64-3.11/numpy/core/src/common -Ibuild/src.linux-sw_64-3.11/numpy/core/src/npymath -c numpy/core/src/npymath/npy_math.c -o build/temp.linux-sw_64-3.11/numpy/core/src/npymath/npy_math.o -MMD -MF build/temp.linux-sw_64-3.11/numpy/core/src/npymath/npy_math.o.d" failed with exit status 1
```

During installation, an error occurred while compiling the unknown CPU model. You need to find the corresponding. h file based on the error. In this. h file, the CPU selection will be determined according to the system architecture during compilation. Shenwei's chip is actually based on the Alpha architecture, and manually adding the processing logic for SW_64 can be compiled and used according to the Alpha architecture. The same applies to other wheels. However, in the end, I failed to install onnx and onnrumtime. You can also consult the official personnel of the Shenwei community. Last year, I gave feedback that Shenwei has not yet integrated into the upstream community, and the adaptation of the ecosystem also requires the joint efforts of operating system manufacturers. I'm not sure if the current system manufacturer provides more support for image sources



---- Replied Message ----
| From | Matti ***@***.***> |
| Date | 09/05/2024 12:09 |
| To | ***@***.***> |
| Cc | ***@***.***>***@***.***> |
| Subject | Re: [numpy/numpy] BUG: Cannot install numpy on UOS system. (Issue #23704) |

@2356852131 please post in English. Google translate gives me

I have encountered the same problem. My server is a domestic server with SW_64 architecture and KylinV10 operating system. Will numpy provide support for SW_64 architecture servers in the future?

Hello, how did you solve it? I also encountered the same problem on SW_64, Kylinv10 server

The answer has not changed: someone with access to that system will need to suggest a fix.

—
Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub, or unsubscribe.

What architecture and compilers does it support? We have undocumented tier support for operating systems: glibc on x86_64 and aarch64, musl x86_64 and arm64, windows, and macOS are explicitly supported. AIX, ppc64 and s390x and others are not officially supported but we will accept patches as long as they seem reasonable. Perhaps you could contribute UOS support if you wish, on a "untested, use at your own risk" basis

I have encountered the same problem，My server is a domestically produced server in China, with an architecture of SW_64 and an operating system of KylinV10. Will numpy provide support for SW_64 architecture servers in the future?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/1956180304/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
